title: Preparing for Exit: Clean-Up, Positioning & the Sale Process
learning_objectives: Build an EBITDA normalization model with defensible and aggressive addbacks
Understand the timeline and process for preparing a company for sale
Model enterprise value impact across different buyer types (strategic vs. PE)
Design an optimal sale process structure to maximize valuation

blocks.fintech_hook: When Thoma Bravo prepared Proofpoint for sale in 2021 — ultimately selling to private equity for $12.3B — they spent 18 months 'cleaning up' the business: rationalizing product lines, restating financials to show pro-forma EBITDA adjustments, resolving pending litigation, and hiring an investment bank 9 months before launch. The preparation added an estimated 1.5x to the exit multiple.
blocks.explanation: Exit preparation follows a structured 18-month timeline leading to close: (1) 18-12 months—financial clean-up (resolve one-time items, normalize EBITDA addbacks, ensure consistent accounting, address any contingent liabilities); (2) 12-9 months—hire financial advisor (investment bank), conduct internal management presentation rehearsal, assess IPO versus M&A optimality; (3) 9-6 months—prepare management presentation and data room (VDR—virtual data room), identify and pre-contact strategic buyers; (4) 6-3 months—launch process (teaser to broad set, CIM to qualified bidders, management presentations, LOIs); (5) 3-0 months—exclusivity with winning bidder, final due diligence, negotiation, signing, closing. EBITDA addbacks represent non-recurring or non-economic items that buyers will not face going forward. Defensible addbacks include: one-time restructuring costs, above-market owner/related-party compensation, material litigation settlements, and IPO preparation costs. Aggressive/indefensible addbacks include: recurring 'one-time' items (red flag for buyers), management perks that buyers will replicate anyway, and synergy-dependent items. The difference between reported EBITDA and adjusted EBITDA can be 30-50% in mature companies, creating substantial valuation impact—a 20pp addback adjustment on a $50M EBITDA business could mean $5-10M of additional enterprise value at a 10-12x multiple. Buyer types (strategic buyers with synergy motivation versus PE buyers evaluating on standalone basis) will have different perspectives on EBITDA normalization defensibility.

code_example.code:
import pandas as pd

# EBITDA Normalization Model
print('EBITDA NORMALIZATION MODEL — EXIT PREPARATION')
print('='*90)

print('\nBaseline Financial Profile:')
print('  Reported Revenue (LTM): $450M')
print('  Reported EBITDA (LTM): $45M')
print('  Reported EBITDA Margin: 10.0%')

# Define Addbacks (Defensible vs. Aggressive)
addbacks = pd.DataFrame({
    'Item': [
        'One-Time Restructuring Costs',
        'Above-Market Owner Compensation',
        'Pending Litigation Settlement',
        'IPO Preparation Costs',
        'Founder Perquisites (cars, travel)',
        'Non-Core Business Wind-Down',
        'TOTAL ADDBACKS'
    ],
    'Amount ($M)': [3.0, 2.0, 1.5, 0.8, 0.5, 1.2, 9.0],
    'Classification': [
        'Defensible',
        'Defensible',
        'Defensible',
        'Defensible',
        'Moderate',
        'Moderate',
        ''
    ],
    'Buyer Perspective': [
        'Won\'t recur; one-time cost',
        'Market rate will be lower',
        'Resolved; no future liability',
        'Not relevant post-close',
        'Buyers replicate; defensible',
        'Efficiency improvement',
        ''
    ],
    'Defense Strength': [
        'High',
        'High',
        'High',
        'High',
        'Medium',
        'Medium',
        ''
    ]
})

print('\n\nEBITDA Addback Schedule:')
print(addbacks[['Item', 'Amount ($M)', 'Classification', 'Buyer Perspective']].to_string(index=False))

print('\n\nADJUSTED EBITDA CALCULATION:')
print('-'*90)
reported_ebitda = 45.0
total_addbacks = 9.0
adjusted_ebitda = reported_ebitda + total_addbacks
adjusted_margin = (adjusted_ebitda / 450) * 100

print(f'Reported EBITDA (LTM): ${reported_ebitda:.1f}M')
print(f'Add: Total Addbacks: ${total_addbacks:.1f}M')
print(f'Adjusted EBITDA: ${adjusted_ebitda:.1f}M')
print(f'Adjusted EBITDA Margin: {adjusted_margin:.1f}%')
print(f'Adjustment Impact: +{(total_addbacks/reported_ebitda)*100:.0f}% improvement')

# Conservative vs. Aggressive Addback Sets
print('\n\n' + '='*90)
print('CONSERVATIVE vs. AGGRESSIVE ADDBACK SCENARIOS')
print('='*90)

scenarios = pd.DataFrame({
    'Addback Item': [
        'Restructuring',
        'Owner Comp',
        'Litigation',
        'IPO Costs',
        'Perquisites',
        'Non-Core',
        'TOTAL',
        'Adjusted EBITDA',
        'Adjusted Margin %'
    ],
    'Conservative': [3.0, 1.5, 1.5, 0.5, 0.2, 0.8, 7.5, 52.5, '11.7%'],
    'Moderate': [3.0, 2.0, 1.5, 0.8, 0.5, 1.2, 9.0, 54.0, '12.0%'],
    'Aggressive': [3.5, 2.5, 1.8, 1.0, 0.8, 1.4, 11.0, 56.0, '12.4%']
})

print('\n' + scenarios.to_string(index=False))

# Valuation Impact
print('\n\n' + '='*90)
print('ENTERPRISE VALUE IMPACT BY SCENARIO')
print('='*90)

multiples = [10, 11, 12]
print('\nEVALUATION AT 10x, 11x, and 12x EBITDA MULTIPLES:\n')

valuation_impact = pd.DataFrame({
    'EBITDA Base': ['Reported', 'Conservative', 'Moderate', 'Aggressive'],
    'EBITDA Amount': [45.0, 52.5, 54.0, 56.0],
    'EV @ 10x': ['$450M', '$525M', '$540M', '$560M'],
    'EV @ 11x': ['$495M', '$577.5M', '$594M', '$616M'],
    'EV @ 12x': ['$540M', '$630M', '$648M', '$672M']
})

print(valuation_impact.to_string(index=False))

print('\n\nVALUATION UPLIFT ANALYSIS:')
print('-'*90)
print('Conservative Addbacks ($7.5M):   $75-90M upside vs. reported (10-12x multiple)')
print('Moderate Addbacks ($9.0M):       $95-108M upside vs. reported (10-12x multiple)')
print('Aggressive Addbacks ($11.0M):    $120-132M upside vs. reported (10-12x multiple)')

print('\n\nDEFENDABILITY ASSESSMENT:')
print('-'*90)
print('CONSERVATIVE: 95%+ defensible — buyers accept with minimal challenge')
print('MODERATE:     80%+ defensible — buyers accept most; may push back on 1-2 items')
print('AGGRESSIVE:   60%+ defensible — significant buyer challenge; discounted multiple likely')

print('\n\nRECOMMENDATION:')
print('-'*90)
print('Target adjusted EBITDA of $54M (12.0% margin) using MODERATE addbacks.')
print('This positions company as ~20% above reported EBITDA with 80%+ buyer acceptance.')
print('Position in CIM: "Adjusted EBITDA of $54M represents normalized ongoing earnings')
print('excluding $9M of non-recurring items and above-market owner costs."')

blocks.scenario: You are managing the 12-month exit preparation process for a cybersecurity portfolio company that Thoma Bravo acquired 5 years ago. Current operating metrics: $420M revenue, $87M reported EBITDA (20.7% margin), $340M enterprise value target (7.8x multiple based on peer comparable). An M&A advisor has identified 6 potential strategic buyers (cybersecurity majors seeking add-on acquisition) and 4 PE firms (financial buyers). Management has submitted an 'aggressive' addback proposal that would adjust EBITDA to $99M (7.8x multiple would yield $772M valuation). You believe their addbacks are 50% indefensible and will face substantial buyer pushback. The dilemma: pushing back on management's addback set risks damaging relationships, but letting aggressive addbacks into the CIM could torpedo deal dynamics when buyers start diligence.
constraints: The CFO is personally invested in the aggressive addback set and views your pushback as questioning his financial integrity; he is considering departure if forced to reduce addbacks
Three of the six strategic buyers have indicated they view aggressive addbacks skeptically based on prior deal experience; they may not engage if the CIM doesn't appear credible
The PE buyer pool is larger and more accepting of addback adjustments; but they will bid lower multiples (6.5-7.0x adjusted EBITDA vs. 8.0-9.0x for strategics)

stakeholder_pressures.pressure: They want to maximize valuation and believe the aggressive addback set ($99M adjusted EBITDA) is justified. They are under LP pressure to deliver 'premium exits' and view the $772M valuation as achievable. They view your pushback as conservative over-caution

They have equity in exit proceeds (5% of equity value). The difference between $340M and $772M valuation is $43.2M to management. They are heavily incentivized to defend aggressive addbacks and may resist 'conservative' positioning

They earn fee based on transaction enterprise value (% of deal size). Higher valuation = higher advisor fee. They favor the aggressive addback set and will argue that 'market will accept it' based on prior cybersecurity deal precedent

blocks.intro: Build the EBITDA normalization model showing conservative vs. aggressive addback sets. Model buyer valuation at strategic (8.5x) and PE (7.0x) multiples for each scenario. Design the optimal sale process to maximize proceeds while maintaining buyer credibility.

steps.instruction: Build the EBITDA normalization model with three addback scenarios: (1) Conservative (only highest-confidence addbacks), (2) Moderate (blended defensible items), (3) Aggressive (management proposal). For each scenario, calculate adjusted EBITDA and apply 8.5x (strategic buyer) and 7.0x (PE buyer) multiples. Show the enterprise value range and identify which scenario maximizes valuation while maintaining buyer credibility.
steps.code:
import pandas as pd

print('CYBERSECURITY PLATFORM: EBITDA NORMALIZATION & VALUATION ANALYSIS')
print('='*100)

print('\nBaseline Metrics:')
print('  Reported Revenue (LTM): $420M')
print('  Reported EBITDA (LTM): $87M (20.7% margin)')
print('  Current Enterprise Value: $340M (3.9x EBITDA)')
print('  Exit Target: 7-9x EBITDA multiple')

# Define Three Addback Scenarios
addbacks_detail = {
    'Restructuring': {'conservative': 4.0, 'moderate': 6.0, 'aggressive': 6.0},
    'Consulting Fees (Thoma Bravo Op)': {'conservative': 1.5, 'moderate': 2.0, 'aggressive': 3.5},
    'Stock-Based Comp (Equity Pool)': {'conservative': 0.5, 'moderate': 1.2, 'aggressive': 2.0},
    'Litigation Settlements': {'conservative': 1.5, 'moderate': 1.5, 'aggressive': 1.5},
    'Real Estate Lease Optimization': {'conservative': 0, 'moderate': 0.8, 'aggressive': 1.8},
    'Management Bonuses (Thoma Bravo)': {'conservative': 0, 'moderate': 0.8, 'aggressive': 2.5},
    'Non-Core Business Divestiture': {'conservative': 1.2, 'moderate': 1.2, 'aggressive': 1.2}
}

scenario_data = []
for item, values in addbacks_detail.items():
    scenario_data.append({
        'Addback Item': item,
        'Conservative': values['conservative'],
        'Moderate': values['moderate'],
        'Aggressive': values['aggressive']
    })

df_addbacks = pd.DataFrame(scenario_data)

print('\n\nADDBACK COMPARISON BY SCENARIO:')
print('-'*100)
print(df_addbacks.to_string(index=False))

# Calculate totals
conservative_total = df_addbacks['Conservative'].sum()
moderate_total = df_addbacks['Moderate'].sum()
aggressive_total = df_addbacks['Aggressive'].sum()

reported_ebitda = 87.0
conservative_adjusted = reported_ebitda + conservative_total
moderate_adjusted = reported_ebitda + moderate_total
aggressive_adjusted = reported_ebitda + aggressive_total

print(f'\n\nEBITDA SUMMARY:' )
print(f'Reported EBITDA: ${reported_ebitda:.1f}M')
print(f'Conservative Addbacks: +${conservative_total:.1f}M → Adjusted EBITDA: ${conservative_adjusted:.1f}M')
print(f'Moderate Addbacks: +${moderate_total:.1f}M → Adjusted EBITDA: ${moderate_adjusted:.1f}M')
print(f'Aggressive Addbacks: +${aggressive_total:.1f}M → Adjusted EBITDA: ${aggressive_adjusted:.1f}M')

# Valuation at Strategic (8.5x) and PE (7.0x) Multiples
print('\n\n' + '='*100)
print('ENTERPRISE VALUE ANALYSIS: STRATEGIC vs. PE BUYER MULTIPLES')
print('='*100)

valuation = pd.DataFrame({
    'Scenario': ['Reported (No addbacks)', 'Conservative', 'Moderate', 'Aggressive'],
    'Adjusted EBITDA ($M)': [reported_ebitda, conservative_adjusted, moderate_adjusted, aggressive_adjusted],
    'EV @ 8.5x (Strategic)': [
        f'${reported_ebitda * 8.5:.0f}M',
        f'${conservative_adjusted * 8.5:.0f}M',
        f'${moderate_adjusted * 8.5:.0f}M',
        f'${aggressive_adjusted * 8.5:.0f}M'
    ],
    'EV @ 7.0x (PE Buyer)': [
        f'${reported_ebitda * 7.0:.0f}M',
        f'${conservative_adjusted * 7.0:.0f}M',
        f'${moderate_adjusted * 7.0:.0f}M',
        f'${aggressive_adjusted * 7.0:.0f}M'
    ],
    'Credibility Rating': [
        '100% (baseline)',
        '95% (buyers accept)',
        '80% (some pushback)',
        '55% (significant challenge)'
    ]
})

print('\n' + valuation.to_string(index=False))

# Strategic vs. PE buyer comparison
print('\n\n' + '='*100)
print('BUYER TYPE ANALYSIS & RECOMMENDED APPROACH')
print('='*100)

buyer_analysis = pd.DataFrame({
    'Buyer Type': ['Strategic (6 identified)', 'PE Firms (4 identified)'],
    'Ideal EBITDA Base': ['Moderate ($95.2M)', 'Conservative ($94.7M)'],
    'Expected Multiple': ['8.5x', '7.0x'],
    'Typical Approach': [
        'Challenge aggressive addbacks; apply 7-8x multiple to adjusted EBITDA after own due diligence adjustments',
        'Accept conservative addbacks; bid 6.5-7.5x multiple based on synergy thesis; less scrutiny on addback details'
    ],
    'Recommended Positioning': [
        'Lead with moderate scenario; emphasize quality of earnings; be prepared to defend each addback',
        'Lead with conservative scenario; emphasize conservatism; opportunity to build credibility and encourage bidding war'
    ]
})

print('\n' + buyer_analysis.to_string(index=False))

print('\n\n' + '='*100)
print('FINANCIAL IMPACT SUMMARY')
print('='*100)

print('\nBase Case Assumption: Win strategic buyer at 8.5x moderate EBITDA')
print(f'Moderate Adjusted EBITDA: ${moderate_adjusted:.1f}M')
print(f'Enterprise Value @ 8.5x: ${moderate_adjusted * 8.5:.0f}M')
print(f'\nDownside Case: Buyer pushes to conservative EBITDA')
print(f'Conservative Adjusted EBITDA: ${conservative_adjusted:.1f}M')
print(f'Enterprise Value @ 8.5x: ${conservative_adjusted * 8.5:.0f}M')
print(f'\nUpside Case: PE buyer accepts moderate EBITDA + adds synergies')
print(f'Moderate Adjusted EBITDA: ${moderate_adjusted:.1f}M')
print(f'Enterprise Value @ 7.5x (with synergy): ${moderate_adjusted * 7.5:.0f}M')
print(f'\nRISK: If aggressive addbacks destroy buyer confidence')
print(f'Scenario: Buyers discount to reported EBITDA + minimal addbacks ($89M)')
print(f'Enterprise Value @ 7.0x: ${89 * 7.0:.0f}M (significant variance from target)')

print('\n\nRECOMMENDATION:')
print('-'*100)
print('Position with MODERATE addbacks ($9.2M, 12.0% adjusted margin)')
print('- Achieves $808M valuation at 8.5x (strategic buyer)')
print('- Maintains 80%+ buyer credibility')
print('- Defensible in diligence conversations')
print('- Maximizes proceeds while reducing deal execution risk')
print('\nManagement Conversation:')
print(f'- Aggressive total ($18.5M): $772M valuation BUT only 55% credibility = delivery risk')
print(f'- Moderate total ($9.2M): $783M valuation with 80% credibility = higher prob of delivery')
print(f'- Net present value of moderate approach is likely higher when accounting for deal risk')

steps.instruction: Model the buyer universe and sale process design. Identify the 6 strategic buyers (with their likely synergy thesis and expected multiple) and 4 PE firms. For the top strategic buyer, estimate their synergy value and compute their price ceiling. Compare the strategic buyer ceiling to PE buyer expected offer and recommend optimal sale process structure (auction vs. negotiated sale).
steps.code:
import pandas as pd

print('BUYER UNIVERSE & SALE PROCESS OPTIMIZATION')
print('='*110)

print('\nTarget Company Profile:')
print('  Revenue: $420M')
print('  Adjusted EBITDA: $95.2M (Moderate scenario)')
print('  Current Portfolio Value: $340M (Thoma Bravo entry)')
print('  Thoma Bravo Hold Period: 5 years')

# Strategic Buyers
strategic_buyers = pd.DataFrame({
    'Strategic Buyer': [
        'CrowdStrike Holdings',
        'Palo Alto Networks',
        'Broadcom (Symantec portfolio)',
        'Fortinet',
        'SentinelOne',
        'Trend Micro'
    ],
    'Motivation': [
        'Expand EDR/XDR platform capabilities',
        'Add to Cortex platform; regional footprint',
        'Fill product gaps in security portfolio',
        'Integrate SMB/enterprise product lines',
        'Platform expansion; customer overlap',
        'Strengthen cloud-native security'
    ],
    'Estimated Synergy': [
        '$12-15M',
        '$10-12M',
        '$8-10M',
        '$7-9M',
        '$9-11M',
        '$6-8M'
    ],
    'Expected Multiple': [
        '10.0-11.0x',
        '9.5-10.5x',
        '8.5-9.5x',
        '8.0-9.0x',
        '8.5-9.5x',
        '8.0-9.0x'
    ],
    'Price Ceiling': [
        '$1,003-1,111M',
        '$952-1,046M',
        '$927-1,021M',
        '$810-906M',
        '$927-1,021M',
        '$762-856M'
    ]
})

print('\n\nSTRATEGIC BUYER UNIVERSE:')
print(strategic_buyers.to_string(index=False))

print('\n\nPE BUYER UNIVERSE:')
pe_buyers = pd.DataFrame({
    'PE Buyer': [
        'Vista Equity Partners',
        'Thoma Bravo (Follow-on)',
        'TPG (Thales/Saab cyber division)',
        'Silver Lake Partners'
    ],
    'Investment Thesis': [
        'Platform business; SaaS margin expansion',
        'Thoma Bravo continuation fund; add-on acquisition play',
        'Build out European cybersecurity portfolio',
        'Growth + margin optimization; tech-focused'
    ],
    'Expected Multiple': [
        '7.0-7.5x',
        '6.5-7.0x',
        '6.5-7.5x',
        '7.0-8.0x'
    ],
    'Price Range': [
        '$651-714M',
        '$619-666M',
        '$619-714M',
        '$666-762M'
    ],
    'Process Preference': [
        'Competitive auction',
        'Negotiated (existing relationship)',
        'Limited auction (3-4 bidders)',
        'Competitive auction'
    ]
})

print(pe_buyers.to_string(index=False))

# Top Strategic Buyer Deep Dive
print('\n\n' + '='*110)
print('DETAILED VALUATION ANALYSIS: TOP STRATEGIC BUYER (CROWDSTRIKE)')
print('='*110)

print('\nCrowdStrike Financial Scenario:')
print('  Standalone EBITDA (Moderate adjustment): $95.2M')
print('  Estimated Synergies (annual): $13.5M')
print('  Pro-forma EBITDA: $108.7M')
print('  Tax Synergies & Other: $1.5M')
print('  Total Realized Value: $110.2M')

synergy_ebitda = 110.2
cs_multiples = {
    'Conservative (10.0x)': synergy_ebitda * 10.0,
    'Base (10.5x)': synergy_ebitda * 10.5,
    'Aggressive (11.0x)': synergy_ebitda * 11.0
}

print('\nValuation at Different Multiple Assumptions:')
for scenario, valuation in cs_multiples.items():
    print(f'  {scenario}: ${valuation:.0f}M')

print('\nCrowdStrike Price Ceiling: $1,102M (at 10.5x with $13.5M synergy)')

# PE Buyer Comparison
print('\n\n' + '='*110)
print('STRATEGIC vs. PE BUYER COMPARISON')
print('='*110)

comparison = pd.DataFrame({
    'Buyer Type': ['Top Strategic (CrowdStrike)', 'Top PE (Vista Equity)', 'Mid PE (Thoma Bravo)'],
    'EBITDA Base': ['$110.2M (incl. synergy)', '$95.2M (standalone)', '$95.2M (standalone)'],
    'Multiple Applied': ['10.5x', '7.5x', '7.0x'],
    'Enterprise Value': ['$1,102M', '$714M', '$666M'],
    'Upside vs. Entry ($340M)': ['+$762M', '+$374M', '+$326M'],
    'Key Advantage': ['Synergy-driven; highest price', 'Capital-efficient; faster close', 'Existing relationship; known partner']
})

print('\n' + comparison.to_string(index=False))

# Recommended Sale Process
print('\n\n' + '='*110)
print('RECOMMENDED SALE PROCESS STRUCTURE')
print('='*110)

print('\nProcess Design: TWO-TRACK AUCTION')
print('\nTrack 1 (Strategic Buyers): Competitive auction with all 6 bidders')
print('  - Launch: Teaser → CIM → Management presentations → Binding LOIs')
print('  - Timeline: 90 days')
print('  - Expected outcome: CrowdStrike or PAN at $1,000-1,100M')
print('\nTrack 2 (PE Buyers): Parallel negotiation with Vista (lead) + TPG (backup)')
print('  - Launch: Concurrent with strategic process')
print('  - Timeline: Aligned with strategic track')
print('  - Expected outcome: $700-750M as fallback / competitive bid')
print('\nProcess Mechanics:')
print('  1. Week 1-2: Market introduction (teaser) to all 10 buyers')
print('  2. Week 3-4: CIM distribution + mgmt presentation schedule')
print('  3. Week 5-6: Management presentations + data room access')
print('  4. Week 7-8: LOI submissions (minimum bid: $800M)')
print('  5. Week 9-12: Exclusivity with winning bidder')
print('  6. Week 13-16: Final diligence, negotiation, signing')
print('  7. Week 17-24: Regulatory approvals, closing')

print('\n\nKEY SUCCESS FACTORS:')
print('-'*110)
print('1. Moderate EBITDA positioning: $95.2M adjusted (80%+ credibility with buyers)')
print('2. Emphasize standalone quality: Focus on organic growth, margins, product leadership')
print('3. Synergy visibility: Help strategic buyers quantify their specific synergy case')
print('4. Competitive tension: Drive auction dynamic between strategic and PE buyers')
print('5. Exit price target: $1,050M (strategic win) vs. $720M fallback (PE buyer)')

print('\n\nPROJECTED PROCEEDS (Before Fees):')
print('-'*110)
print('Expected exit: $1,050M (CrowdStrike at 10.5x pro-forma EBITDA)')
print('Less: Thoma Bravo basis (debt): ~$200M')
print('Equity proceeds: ~$850M')
print('Thoma Bravo fund ownership (estimate): 50% = $425M fund return')
print('\nReturn Multiples: 5.0-5.5x invested capital (typical for successful tech PE exit)')
